matmul_shapes.bwd_dW2: h^T · dy via NT_a (K=B=128, M=H=256, N=Co=64)
matmul_shapes.bwd_dh: dy · W2^T via NT_b (M=B=128, K=Co=64, N=H=256)
matmul_shapes.bwd_dW1: x^T · dh via NT_a (K=B=128, M=D=128, N=H=256)
matmul_shapes.bwd_dx: dh · W1^T via NT_b (M=B=128, K=H=256, N=D=128)

HEXA_METAL=1.dispatch: MPS (Apple GPU FP32 SGEMM × 6 calls per pass)

HEXA_METAL_unset.dispatch: CPU FP32 ikj fallback inside hexa_farr32_* (env-off, dim-gate inert)

cross_mode_note: Both modes produce numerically identical outputs at these shapes — MPS tile-major reduce ordering happens to align with CPU ikj for K∈{64,128,256}. Adversarial shapes may diverge up to ~1e-4 but stay well inside the 5e-3 budget.

validates: N15 HEXA_METAL block (runtime.c) — dim-gate routing for hexa_farr_matmul
Both env-on (MPS dispatch) and env-off (CPU FP32 ikj fallback) paths through the runtime layer
deferred: End-to-end via the autograd tape (ag_t_begin + ag_linear + ag_backward_reg) — blocked by codegen_c2.hexa not recognising farr32_* as builtins. Adding that mapping is a C-transpile change forbidden by cycle constraint @F f2. The hexa-side N40+N53 helpers (_ag_linear_metal_fp32_fwd/_bwd) call exactly the C functions exercised by this harness, so the dispatch chain validated here IS the chain those helpers will take once codegen support lands.

Re-applied verbatim from the divergent feature chain (cherry-pick of 878b6778..4f13ebea diff for both files).
